_comment: Curated, validated input to `cargo xtask gen`. Joined with the clang C inventory (c-api.json) and the syn-extracted Rust inventory to render docs/generated/port-parity*.md. Every counterpart's rust symbol must exist in the Rust inventory and every referenced court must exist as a reports/oracle/<id>.json receipt; `cargo xtask check` enforces both.

legend.meaning: every function in the group reconstructed and admitted by a court
some behavior reconstructed with an executable court (byte/terminfo oracle)
a byte-producing stand-in exists, not yet pinned by its own court
a counterpart exists but a court records a measured divergence from the in-repo oracle
no immediate terminal output; the function's effect is realized later by refresh/doupdate, proven by an empty-output court
no terminal-output contract (pure query or state); nothing for a byte-output reconstruction to emit

coverage_definition: A function is 'resolved' when it has a counterpart (full/partial/scaffold/divergent) OR a non-output classification (deferred/n_a) backed by evidence. Resolution is NOT a claim of API equivalence: ncurses-native exposes byte producers, not the ncurses C function signatures. Counterparts marked full/partial/divergent are linked to oracle receipts; scaffold counterparts are honest stand-ins; deferred functions are proven to emit no immediate bytes (their effect is a later refresh); n_a functions are pure queries with no terminal-output contract.

clearok.note: Sets the force-clear flag and emits nothing; the next refresh emits clear_screen.

is_cleared.note: Pure query returning the clearok flag; no terminal-output contract for a byte reconstruction to emit.

overlay.note: Copies non-blank cells from one window to another in memory and emits nothing; the result is shown by a later refresh.

overwrite.note: Copies all cells from one window to another in memory and emits nothing; effect deferred to refresh.

copywin.note: Rectangle copy between windows in memory; emits nothing, effect deferred to refresh.

del_curterm.note: Manages/frees the global cur_term; ncurses-native uses owned Terminfo values, so there is no global pointer to set/delete/free and no terminal output to emit.

del_curterm_sp.note: Manages/frees the global cur_term; ncurses-native uses owned Terminfo values, so there is no global pointer to set/delete/free and no terminal output to emit.

set_curterm.note: Manages/frees the global cur_term; ncurses-native uses owned Terminfo values, so there is no global pointer to set/delete/free and no terminal output to emit.

set_curterm_sp.note: Manages/frees the global cur_term; ncurses-native uses owned Terminfo values, so there is no global pointer to set/delete/free and no terminal output to emit.

exit_terminfo.note: Manages/frees the global cur_term; ncurses-native uses owned Terminfo values, so there is no global pointer to set/delete/free and no terminal output to emit.

tiscan_s.note: Scans a format string for its parameter count/types (a safety/introspection helper); produces no terminal output to reconstruct.

redrawwin.note: Marks a window/line range for full repaint on the next refresh; emits no immediate terminal bytes (proven empty-output).

wredrawln.note: Marks a window/line range for full repaint on the next refresh; emits no immediate terminal bytes (proven empty-output).

bkgd.note: Sets a window's background char+attribute in memory; emits no immediate terminal bytes (shown by a later refresh). Proven empty-output.

bkgdset.note: Sets a window's background char+attribute in memory; emits no immediate terminal bytes (shown by a later refresh). Proven empty-output.

wbkgd.note: Sets a window's background char+attribute in memory; emits no immediate terminal bytes (shown by a later refresh). Proven empty-output.

wbkgdset.note: Sets a window's background char+attribute in memory; emits no immediate terminal bytes (shown by a later refresh). Proven empty-output.

getbkgd.note: Pure query returning a window's background char+attribute; no terminal-output contract.

termattrs.note: Supported-video-attributes mask computed at the SCREEN(SP) level (0 without a screen); requires the screen model not present in the terminfo-only substrate.

termattrs_sp.note: Supported-video-attributes mask computed at the SCREEN(SP) level (0 without a screen); requires the screen model not present in the terminfo-only substrate.

baudrate.note: Queries the live terminal's tty/termios state (baud / erase / kill char), not terminfo; no terminal-output contract for a byte reconstruction.

baudrate_sp.note: Queries the live terminal's tty/termios state (baud / erase / kill char), not terminfo; no terminal-output contract for a byte reconstruction.

erasechar.note: Queries the live terminal's tty/termios state (baud / erase / kill char), not terminfo; no terminal-output contract for a byte reconstruction.

killchar.note: Queries the live terminal's tty/termios state (baud / erase / kill char), not terminfo; no terminal-output contract for a byte reconstruction.

killchar_sp.note: Queries the live terminal's tty/termios state (baud / erase / kill char), not terminfo; no terminal-output contract for a byte reconstruction.

curses_version.note: Returns the curses library identity/version string; not a terminal capability and no terminal-output contract.

touchwin.note: Marks window lines dirty/in-sync in memory; emits no immediate terminal bytes (repaint is the next refresh's job). Proven empty-output.

touchline.note: Marks window lines dirty/in-sync in memory; emits no immediate terminal bytes (repaint is the next refresh's job). Proven empty-output.

untouchwin.note: Marks window lines dirty/in-sync in memory; emits no immediate terminal bytes (repaint is the next refresh's job). Proven empty-output.

wcursyncup.note: Marks window lines dirty/in-sync in memory; emits no immediate terminal bytes (repaint is the next refresh's job). Proven empty-output.

syncok.note: Marks window lines dirty/in-sync in memory; emits no immediate terminal bytes (repaint is the next refresh's job). Proven empty-output.

is_linetouched.note: Pure query of window touch/sync state; no terminal-output contract.

is_wintouched.note: Pure query of window touch/sync state; no terminal-output contract.

is_syncok.note: Pure query of window touch/sync state; no terminal-output contract.

scrollok.note: Sets an output-option flag / scroll region, or scrolls window contents in memory; emits no immediate terminal bytes (output at the next refresh). Proven empty-output.

idlok.note: Sets an output-option flag / scroll region, or scrolls window contents in memory; emits no immediate terminal bytes (output at the next refresh). Proven empty-output.

get_escdelay_sp.note: Pure query of the ESC-sequence read delay; no terminal-output contract.

delay_output.note: Output-timing delay; like tputs padding it emits no bytes on the admitted xterm/normal-baud environment.

delay_output_sp.note: Output-timing delay; like tputs padding it emits no bytes on the admitted xterm/normal-baud environment.

mcprint.note: Sends data to the printer via print-mode caps; targets a printer device, not the terminal display (xterm has no printer caps).

mcprint_sp.note: Sends data to the printer via print-mode caps; targets a printer device, not the terminal display (xterm has no printer caps).

delwin.note: Frees a window; ncurses-native uses owned Window values (dropped normally), so there is nothing to emit.

wgetparent.note: Returns the parent window pointer; a pointer query with no terminal-output contract.

start_color.note: Initializes the colour subsystem; no immediate terminal output.

start_color_sp.note: Initializes the colour subsystem; no immediate terminal output.

use_default_colors.note: Configures default (-1) colours; library state with no immediate terminal output.

use_default_colors_sp.note: Configures default (-1) colours; library state with no immediate terminal output.

_traceattr.note: Writes diagnostic trace to the ncurses debug file (when tracing is enabled), not the terminal display; no terminal-output contract.

_traceattr2.note: Writes diagnostic trace to the ncurses debug file (when tracing is enabled), not the terminal display; no terminal-output contract.

trace.note: Writes diagnostic trace to the ncurses debug file (when tracing is enabled), not the terminal display; no terminal-output contract.

resizeterm.note: Resizes the screen/window structures in memory; emits no immediate terminal bytes (repaint at the next refresh). Proven empty-output.

resizeterm_sp.note: Resizes the screen/window structures in memory; emits no immediate terminal bytes (repaint at the next refresh). Proven empty-output.

use_window.note: Thread wrapper that runs a callback under a window/screen lock; no terminal-output contract of its own.

mousemask.note: Sets the reported mouse-event mask and enables tracking at runtime; the tracking sequence is emitted by a later refresh.

mouseinterval.note: Gets/sets the click-resolution interval; runtime config with no terminal output.

has_mouse.note: Runtime query: whether a mouse is currently enabled/detected (depends on mousemask), not a terminfo-only property.

has_mouse_sp.note: Runtime query: whether a mouse is currently enabled/detected (depends on mousemask), not a terminfo-only property.

slk_noutrefresh.note: Touch/clear/restore/refresh of the label line emit no immediate terminal bytes; the line is rendered at the buffered doupdate (a seed). Proven empty-output.

slk_noutrefresh_sp.note: Touch/clear/restore/refresh of the label line emit no immediate terminal bytes; the line is rendered at the buffered doupdate (a seed). Proven empty-output.

slk_clear.note: Touch/clear/restore/refresh of the label line emit no immediate terminal bytes; the line is rendered at the buffered doupdate (a seed). Proven empty-output.

slk_clear_sp.note: Touch/clear/restore/refresh of the label line emit no immediate terminal bytes; the line is rendered at the buffered doupdate (a seed). Proven empty-output.

slk_restore.note: Touch/clear/restore/refresh of the label line emit no immediate terminal bytes; the line is rendered at the buffered doupdate (a seed). Proven empty-output.

slk_restore_sp.note: Touch/clear/restore/refresh of the label line emit no immediate terminal bytes; the line is rendered at the buffered doupdate (a seed). Proven empty-output.

slk_refresh.note: Touch/clear/restore/refresh of the label line emit no immediate terminal bytes; the line is rendered at the buffered doupdate (a seed). Proven empty-output.

slk_refresh_sp.note: Touch/clear/restore/refresh of the label line emit no immediate terminal bytes; the line is rendered at the buffered doupdate (a seed). Proven empty-output.

slk_init.note: Selects the soft-label format before initscr; layout config with no terminal output.

slk_init_sp.note: Selects the soft-label format before initscr; layout config with no terminal output.

slk_attr.note: Soft-label attribute/colour state; no immediate terminal output (applied when the label line is rendered).

slk_attr_sp.note: Soft-label attribute/colour state; no immediate terminal output (applied when the label line is rendered).

slk_attr_off.note: Soft-label attribute/colour state; no immediate terminal output (applied when the label line is rendered).

slk_attr_on.note: Soft-label attribute/colour state; no immediate terminal output (applied when the label line is rendered).

slk_attr_set.note: Soft-label attribute/colour state; no immediate terminal output (applied when the label line is rendered).

slk_attr_set_sp.note: Soft-label attribute/colour state; no immediate terminal output (applied when the label line is rendered).

slk_attroff.note: Soft-label attribute/colour state; no immediate terminal output (applied when the label line is rendered).

slk_attroff_sp.note: Soft-label attribute/colour state; no immediate terminal output (applied when the label line is rendered).

slk_attron.note: Soft-label attribute/colour state; no immediate terminal output (applied when the label line is rendered).

slk_attron_sp.note: Soft-label attribute/colour state; no immediate terminal output (applied when the label line is rendered).

slk_attrset.note: Soft-label attribute/colour state; no immediate terminal output (applied when the label line is rendered).

slk_attrset_sp.note: Soft-label attribute/colour state; no immediate terminal output (applied when the label line is rendered).

slk_color.note: Soft-label attribute/colour state; no immediate terminal output (applied when the label line is rendered).

slk_color_sp.note: Soft-label attribute/colour state; no immediate terminal output (applied when the label line is rendered).

init_color.note: Redefines a colour's rgb; requires can_change_color (false on the admitted xterm), so it is a no-op with no terminal output.

init_color_sp.note: Redefines a colour's rgb; requires can_change_color (false on the admitted xterm), so it is a no-op with no terminal output.

init_extended_color.note: Redefines a colour's rgb; requires can_change_color (false on the admitted xterm), so it is a no-op with no terminal output.

init_extended_color_sp.note: Redefines a colour's rgb; requires can_change_color (false on the admitted xterm), so it is a no-op with no terminal output.

extended_slk_color.note: Soft-label colour (extended); label-line attribute state, no immediate terminal output.

extended_slk_color_sp.note: Soft-label colour (extended); label-line attribute state, no immediate terminal output.

getwin.note: Serializes/loads a window or screen to/from a file in ncurses' private save format; not terminal-display output (the crate's scope).

getwin_sp.note: Serializes/loads a window or screen to/from a file in ncurses' private save format; not terminal-display output (the crate's scope).

putwin.note: Serializes/loads a window or screen to/from a file in ncurses' private save format; not terminal-display output (the crate's scope).

scr_dump.note: Serializes/loads a window or screen to/from a file in ncurses' private save format; not terminal-display output (the crate's scope).

scr_init.note: Serializes/loads a window or screen to/from a file in ncurses' private save format; not terminal-display output (the crate's scope).

scr_init_sp.note: Serializes/loads a window or screen to/from a file in ncurses' private save format; not terminal-display output (the crate's scope).

scr_restore.note: Serializes/loads a window or screen to/from a file in ncurses' private save format; not terminal-display output (the crate's scope).

scr_restore_sp.note: Serializes/loads a window or screen to/from a file in ncurses' private save format; not terminal-display output (the crate's scope).

scr_set.note: Serializes/loads a window or screen to/from a file in ncurses' private save format; not terminal-display output (the crate's scope).

scr_set_sp.note: Serializes/loads a window or screen to/from a file in ncurses' private save format; not terminal-display output (the crate's scope).

beep.note: Emits the bel capability (xterm: 0x07); a pure output cap with no window state.

beep_sp.note: Byte-identical to beep; the _sp screen selection is not modeled but emits the same bel byte.

flash.note: Emits the flash capability; the $<100/> delay is a real-time pause, not bytes, so the stream is the DECSCNM on/off pair.

flash_sp.note: Byte-identical to flash; the _sp screen selection is not modeled.

tigetstr.note: Reconstructed from a native compiled-terminfo reader; matches ncurses tigetstr over every standard string cap of the xterm entry (return semantics: present/absent/not-a-string).

tigetstr_sp.note: Same lookup; the _sp screen-pointer global state is not modeled but the returned value is identical.

tigetnum.note: Reconstructed terminfo numeric lookup; matches ncurses tigetnum over every standard numeric cap (value / -1 absent / -2 not-a-number).

tigetnum_sp.note: Same lookup; _sp variant not modeled, value identical.

tigetflag.note: Reconstructed terminfo boolean lookup; matches ncurses tigetflag over every standard boolean cap (1 / 0 / -1 not-a-boolean).

tigetflag_sp.note: Same lookup; _sp variant not modeled, value identical.

tparm.note: Full parameterized-string stack machine; matches ncurses tparm byte-for-byte over the xterm parameterized caps (cup/setaf/sgr/setf/...) and synthetic printf specs. Padding ($<...>) is tputs' job; static-variable persistence across calls not modeled.

tparm_sp.note: Same evaluator; the _sp screen-pointer variant is not modeled but the bytes are identical.

tiparm.note: Shares the tparm evaluator (byte output identical for these caps); tiparm's explicit-typed variadic API is not separately reconstructed/courted.

tputs.note: Padding processor: strips $<...> specs; matches ncurses tputs byte-for-byte under the admitted xterm pty. Baud/pad_char-dependent pad bytes on slow terminals not modeled (non-claim).

tputs_sp.note: Same processing; _sp screen-pointer variant not modeled, bytes identical.

putp.note: tputs to stdout (affcnt 1); emitted bytes are exactly tputs of the string.

putp_sp.note: Same as putp; _sp variant not modeled, bytes identical.

mvcur.note: Clean-room reproduction of lib_mvcur.c's onscreen_mvcur tactic enumeration scored by relative_move with the static sample-parameter cost model from _nc_mvcur_init (strict-< tie-break, ovw=FALSE per public mvcur(3)). Verified byte-exact against real libncurses for ALL 23409 pairs of a 153x153 sampled (from -> to) grid on an 80x24 xterm pty (court NCURSES.MVCUR); the offline replay is tests/mvcur_matrix.rs.

mvcur_sp.note: Same byte producer; the screen-pointer (_sp) reentrant variant is not modeled.

clear.note: Emits the clear_screen capability bytes; not the full clear() window-state semantics.

wclear.note: Same clear_screen bytes; no WINDOW model.

wclrtoeol.note: Same el bytes; no WINDOW model.

wclrtobot.note: Same ed bytes; no WINDOW model.

color_set.note: Sets the active colour pair on the cell grid; matched to ncurses via winch colour bits.

wcolor_set.note: Sets the active colour pair on the cell grid; matched to ncurses via winch colour bits.

initscr.note: Emits the init prologue; against the in-repo ncurses 6.4 pty oracle the composite framing diverges (emission order + feature extras), recorded not matched.

endwin.note: Emits the teardown epilogue; same recorded framing divergence as initscr against the in-repo oracle.

init_pair.note: Registers a colour pair; pair_content matched to ncurses.

attron.note: Turns attribute bits on for subsequent writes; cell attributes matched to ncurses A_* via winch.

attroff.note: Turns attribute bits off; cell attributes matched to ncurses via winch.

attrset.note: Sets the active attributes; cell attributes matched to ncurses via winch.

wattron.note: Turns attribute bits on for subsequent writes; cell attributes matched to ncurses A_* via winch.

wattroff.note: Turns attribute bits off; cell attributes matched to ncurses via winch.

wattrset.note: Sets the active attributes; cell attributes matched to ncurses via winch.

standout.note: Turns on A_STANDOUT; matched via winch.

standend.note: Turns all attributes off; matched via winch.

doupdate.note: Clean-room reproduction of tty_update.c (doupdate/ClrUpdate/TransformLine/PutRange/EmitRange/ClrToEOL/ClrBottom) for the plain-text (A_NORMAL, no color) path, reusing the byte-exact mvcur/mvcur_ovw for cursor motion. Byte-exact vs real ncurses on ALL 19 court scenarios (NCURSES.DOUPDATE): clear+paint, overwrite, multi-line, scattered runs, leading-blank reposition, clr_eol/clr_bol/clr_eos, ich/dch insert/delete shifting, ech and rep run-coalescing, and the mvcur ovw=TRUE GoTo overwrite (OPT-01a closed). Attribute/color SGR (OPT-03), xmc, bce, hardware scroll, and the right-margin wrap corner are out of this increment.

refresh.note: wrefresh(stdscr) = wnoutrefresh + doupdate; the doupdate plain-text screen-diff is reproduced byte-exact for the courted scenarios (NCURSES.DOUPDATE). The wnoutrefresh window-to-newscr copy and pad/sub-window clipping are modeled by the Window cell grid, not yet courted end-to-end.

wnoutrefresh.note: The newscr assembly feeds the same byte-exact doupdate diff (NCURSES.DOUPDATE). Full WINDOW-to-newscr copy semantics (clipping, leaveok, immedok) are partial.

wrefresh.note: wnoutrefresh + doupdate; the doupdate plain-text screen-diff is byte-exact for the courted scenarios (NCURSES.DOUPDATE), reusing the byte-exact mvcur.

setupterm.note: Reconstructs the load-by-$TERM + compiled-entry parse path; matched against ncurses across 9 terminals (incl. the 32-bit-number format). Runtime cur_term/baud/mode setup and use_env cols/lines augmentation are not modeled (non-claim).

restartterm.note: Re-reads the entry for a term name via the same loader; full restartterm tty/mode restoration is not modeled.

restartterm_sp.note: Re-reads the entry for a term name via the same loader; full restartterm tty/mode restoration is not modeled.

tgetflag.note: Termcap boolean lookup by 2-letter code; matches ncurses (44/44 codes).

tgetflag_sp.note: Termcap boolean lookup by 2-letter code; matches ncurses (44/44 codes).

tgetnum.note: Termcap numeric lookup by code; matches ncurses (39/39 codes).

tgetnum_sp.note: Termcap numeric lookup by code; matches ncurses (39/39 codes).

tgetstr.note: Returns the terminfo string for a termcap code; matches 411/414. Diverges on the few codes ncurses' termcap emulation massages/synthesizes (e.g. 'me' -> \E[0m vs raw sgr0; 'ML' margin cap). Recorded in NCURSES.TERMCAP.

tgetstr_sp.note: Returns the terminfo string for a termcap code; matches 411/414. Diverges on the few codes ncurses' termcap emulation massages/synthesizes (e.g. 'me' -> \E[0m vs raw sgr0; 'ML' margin cap). Recorded in NCURSES.TERMCAP.

tgoto.note: tgoto(cap,col,row) == tparm(cap,row,col); matches ncurses over the cup sweep.

tgoto_sp.note: tgoto(cap,col,row) == tparm(cap,row,col); matches ncurses over the cup sweep.

tgetent.note: Loads the entry by name via the database loader; full tgetent buffer/area semantics and termcap-file fallback not modeled.

tgetent_sp.note: Loads the entry by name via the database loader; full tgetent buffer/area semantics and termcap-file fallback not modeled.

tiparm_s.note: Bounded-buffer variant of tiparm; shares the tparm evaluator (identical bytes for these caps). The explicit buffer-size-limit API is not separately modelled.

doupdate_sp.note: Same single-shape repaint seed as doupdate; the _sp screen-pointer variant is not modelled.

longname.note: Verbose terminal description (last alias); matches ncurses across 9 terminals.

longname_sp.note: Verbose terminal description (last alias); matches ncurses across 9 terminals.

termname.note: Primary terminal name (first alias, <=14 bytes); matches ncurses across 9 terminals.

termname_sp.note: Primary terminal name (first alias, <=14 bytes); matches ncurses across 9 terminals.

has_ic.note: Insert-character capability derived from terminfo; matches ncurses across 9 terminals.

has_ic_sp.note: Insert-character capability derived from terminfo; matches ncurses across 9 terminals.

has_il.note: Insert/delete-line capability derived from terminfo; matches ncurses across 9 terminals.

has_il_sp.note: Insert/delete-line capability derived from terminfo; matches ncurses across 9 terminals.

curs_set.note: Cursor-visibility selection reconstructed from terminfo (civis/cnorm/cvvis via tputs); matches ncurses' resolved caps.

curs_set_sp.note: Cursor-visibility selection reconstructed from terminfo (civis/cnorm/cvvis via tputs); matches ncurses' resolved caps.

addch.note: Writes a printable char/newline into the window cell grid (wrap/clear-to-EOL/clip); state matched to ncurses by winch readback.

waddch.note: Writes a printable char/newline into the window cell grid (wrap/clear-to-EOL/clip); state matched to ncurses by winch readback.

mvaddch.note: Writes a printable char/newline into the window cell grid (wrap/clear-to-EOL/clip); state matched to ncurses by winch readback.

mvwaddch.note: Writes a printable char/newline into the window cell grid (wrap/clear-to-EOL/clip); state matched to ncurses by winch readback.

echochar.note: Add reconstructed in the cell grid; the echo's immediate refresh is the doupdate seed (not separately courted).

wechochar.note: Add reconstructed in the cell grid; the echo's immediate refresh is the doupdate seed (not separately courted).

addstr.note: Adds a byte string into the cell grid via addch; state matched to ncurses by winch readback. The n-variants' length limit is the caller's slice.

waddstr.note: Adds a byte string into the cell grid via addch; state matched to ncurses by winch readback. The n-variants' length limit is the caller's slice.

mvaddstr.note: Adds a byte string into the cell grid via addch; state matched to ncurses by winch readback. The n-variants' length limit is the caller's slice.

mvwaddstr.note: Adds a byte string into the cell grid via addch; state matched to ncurses by winch readback. The n-variants' length limit is the caller's slice.

addnstr.note: Adds a byte string into the cell grid via addch; state matched to ncurses by winch readback. The n-variants' length limit is the caller's slice.

waddnstr.note: Adds a byte string into the cell grid via addch; state matched to ncurses by winch readback. The n-variants' length limit is the caller's slice.

mvaddnstr.note: Adds a byte string into the cell grid via addch; state matched to ncurses by winch readback. The n-variants' length limit is the caller's slice.

mvwaddnstr.note: Adds a byte string into the cell grid via addch; state matched to ncurses by winch readback. The n-variants' length limit is the caller's slice.

inch.note: Reads a cell's character from the window grid; the court reads ncurses back the same way. Attribute/chtype bits are not modelled.

winch.note: Reads a cell's character from the window grid; the court reads ncurses back the same way. Attribute/chtype bits are not modelled.

mvinch.note: Reads a cell's character from the window grid; the court reads ncurses back the same way. Attribute/chtype bits are not modelled.

mvwinch.note: Reads a cell's character from the window grid; the court reads ncurses back the same way. Attribute/chtype bits are not modelled.

move.note: Sets the window's logical cursor position; emits no bytes and the resulting position is matched to ncurses by the window-state court.

wmove.note: Sets the window's logical cursor position; emits no bytes and the resulting position is matched to ncurses by the window-state court.

erase.note: Clears the window cell grid to blanks; emits no immediate bytes and the resulting grid is matched to ncurses by the window-state court.

werase.note: Clears the window cell grid to blanks; emits no immediate bytes and the resulting grid is matched to ncurses by the window-state court.

insch.note: Inserts a char at the cursor, shifting the line right (last col lost); state matched by winch readback.

winsch.note: Inserts a char at the cursor, shifting the line right (last col lost); state matched by winch readback.

mvinsch.note: Inserts a char at the cursor, shifting the line right (last col lost); state matched by winch readback.

mvwinsch.note: Inserts a char at the cursor, shifting the line right (last col lost); state matched by winch readback.

insstr.note: Inserts a string at the cursor (line shifts right; cursor stays); state matched by winch readback. The n-variants' limit is the caller's slice.

insnstr.note: Inserts a string at the cursor (line shifts right; cursor stays); state matched by winch readback. The n-variants' limit is the caller's slice.

winsstr.note: Inserts a string at the cursor (line shifts right; cursor stays); state matched by winch readback. The n-variants' limit is the caller's slice.

winsnstr.note: Inserts a string at the cursor (line shifts right; cursor stays); state matched by winch readback. The n-variants' limit is the caller's slice.

mvinsstr.note: Inserts a string at the cursor (line shifts right; cursor stays); state matched by winch readback. The n-variants' limit is the caller's slice.

mvinsnstr.note: Inserts a string at the cursor (line shifts right; cursor stays); state matched by winch readback. The n-variants' limit is the caller's slice.

mvwinsstr.note: Inserts a string at the cursor (line shifts right; cursor stays); state matched by winch readback. The n-variants' limit is the caller's slice.

mvwinsnstr.note: Inserts a string at the cursor (line shifts right; cursor stays); state matched by winch readback. The n-variants' limit is the caller's slice.

delch.note: Deletes the char at the cursor, shifting the line left (last col blank); state matched by winch readback.

wdelch.note: Deletes the char at the cursor, shifting the line left (last col blank); state matched by winch readback.

mvdelch.note: Deletes the char at the cursor, shifting the line left (last col blank); state matched by winch readback.

mvwdelch.note: Deletes the char at the cursor, shifting the line left (last col blank); state matched by winch readback.

deleteln.note: Deletes the cursor line, shifting lower lines up (bottom blank); state matched by winch readback.

wdeleteln.note: Deletes the cursor line, shifting lower lines up (bottom blank); state matched by winch readback.

insertln.note: Inserts a blank line at the cursor row, shifting lower lines down (bottom lost); state matched by winch readback.

winsertln.note: Inserts a blank line at the cursor row, shifting lower lines down (bottom lost); state matched by winch readback.

insdelln.note: Inserts/deletes n lines at the cursor row; state matched by winch readback.

winsdelln.note: Inserts/deletes n lines at the cursor row; state matched by winch readback.

instr.note: Reads characters from the cursor to end of row; the cells it returns are validated by the window-state court.

innstr.note: Reads characters from the cursor to end of row; the cells it returns are validated by the window-state court.

winstr.note: Reads characters from the cursor to end of row; the cells it returns are validated by the window-state court.

winnstr.note: Reads characters from the cursor to end of row; the cells it returns are validated by the window-state court.

mvinstr.note: Reads characters from the cursor to end of row; the cells it returns are validated by the window-state court.

mvinnstr.note: Reads characters from the cursor to end of row; the cells it returns are validated by the window-state court.

mvwinstr.note: Reads characters from the cursor to end of row; the cells it returns are validated by the window-state court.

mvwinnstr.note: Reads characters from the cursor to end of row; the cells it returns are validated by the window-state court.

inchstr.note: Char cells reconstructed (via instr); the chtype attribute bits these return are not modelled.

inchnstr.note: Char cells reconstructed (via instr); the chtype attribute bits these return are not modelled.

winchstr.note: Char cells reconstructed (via instr); the chtype attribute bits these return are not modelled.

winchnstr.note: Char cells reconstructed (via instr); the chtype attribute bits these return are not modelled.

mvinchstr.note: Char cells reconstructed (via instr); the chtype attribute bits these return are not modelled.

mvinchnstr.note: Char cells reconstructed (via instr); the chtype attribute bits these return are not modelled.

mvwinchstr.note: Char cells reconstructed (via instr); the chtype attribute bits these return are not modelled.

mvwinchnstr.note: Char cells reconstructed (via instr); the chtype attribute bits these return are not modelled.

border.note: Draws a border into the cell grid (sides + corners, 0 = ACS default); state matched by winch readback.

wborder.note: Draws a border into the cell grid (sides + corners, 0 = ACS default); state matched by winch readback.

box.note: Border with given side chars and default ACS corners; state matched by winch readback.

hline.note: Draws a horizontal line of n chars (0 = ACS_HLINE), clipped at the right edge; state matched by winch readback.

whline.note: Draws a horizontal line of n chars (0 = ACS_HLINE), clipped at the right edge; state matched by winch readback.

mvhline.note: Draws a horizontal line of n chars (0 = ACS_HLINE), clipped at the right edge; state matched by winch readback.

mvwhline.note: Draws a horizontal line of n chars (0 = ACS_HLINE), clipped at the right edge; state matched by winch readback.

vline.note: Draws a vertical line of n chars (0 = ACS_VLINE), clipped at the bottom; state matched by winch readback.

wvline.note: Draws a vertical line of n chars (0 = ACS_VLINE), clipped at the bottom; state matched by winch readback.

mvvline.note: Draws a vertical line of n chars (0 = ACS_VLINE), clipped at the bottom; state matched by winch readback.

mvwvline.note: Draws a vertical line of n chars (0 = ACS_VLINE), clipped at the bottom; state matched by winch readback.

newwin.note: Creates a blank window of given size at a screen position; geometry matched by the geometry court.

newwin_sp.note: Same as newwin; the _sp screen-pointer variant is not modelled.

mvwin.note: Moves the window's top-left corner; geometry matched by the court.

wresize.note: Resizes the window (preserving top-left content); geometry matched by the court.

dupwin.note: Copies cells, cursor, and geometry; matched by the geometry/state courts.

derwin.note: Child window at a parent-relative position; geometry (begyx/paryx) matched by the court. Shared parent cells are not modelled.

subwin.note: Child window at a screen-absolute position; geometry matched by the court. Shared parent cells are not modelled.

mvderwin.note: The derived-window view shift is not modelled; only child geometry via derwin.

is_subwin.note: True when the window has a parent; matched by the geometry court.

getbegx.note: Window top-left screen position; matched by the geometry court.

getbegy.note: Window top-left screen position; matched by the geometry court.

getmaxx.note: Window size; matched by the geometry court.

getmaxy.note: Window size; matched by the geometry court.

getcurx.note: Window cursor position; matched by the geometry/window-state courts.

getcury.note: Window cursor position; matched by the geometry/window-state courts.

getparx.note: Position relative to the parent window; matched by the geometry court.

getpary.note: Position relative to the parent window; matched by the geometry court.

attr_on.note: Turns attribute bits on for subsequent writes; cell attributes matched to ncurses A_* via winch.

wattr_on.note: Turns attribute bits on for subsequent writes; cell attributes matched to ncurses A_* via winch.

attr_off.note: Turns attribute bits off; cell attributes matched to ncurses via winch.

wattr_off.note: Turns attribute bits off; cell attributes matched to ncurses via winch.

attr_set.note: Sets the active attributes; cell attributes matched to ncurses via winch.

wattr_set.note: Sets the active attributes; cell attributes matched to ncurses via winch.

chgat.note: Changes the attributes of n cells at the cursor (chars unchanged); matched to ncurses via winch.

wchgat.note: Changes the attributes of n cells at the cursor (chars unchanged); matched to ncurses via winch.

mvchgat.note: Changes the attributes of n cells at the cursor (chars unchanged); matched to ncurses via winch.

mvwchgat.note: Changes the attributes of n cells at the cursor (chars unchanged); matched to ncurses via winch.

wstandout.note: Turns on A_STANDOUT; matched via winch.

wstandend.note: Turns all attributes off; matched via winch.

vidattr.note: Clean-room reproduction of vidputs/_nc_do_color (the SGR attribute + color-pair transition state machine) in vid::Vid, built on the byte-exact tparm and the color Palette. Verified byte-exact against real ncurses for bold/underline/reverse and the ANSI color pairs, integrated into and courted via doupdate (NCURSES.DOUPDATE). Italic/altcharset/xmc/ncv and use_default_colors are not modeled.

vidattr_sp.note: Clean-room reproduction of vidputs/_nc_do_color (the SGR attribute + color-pair transition state machine) in vid::Vid, built on the byte-exact tparm and the color Palette. Verified byte-exact against real ncurses for bold/underline/reverse and the ANSI color pairs, integrated into and courted via doupdate (NCURSES.DOUPDATE). Italic/altcharset/xmc/ncv and use_default_colors are not modeled.

vidputs.note: Clean-room reproduction of vidputs/_nc_do_color (the SGR attribute + color-pair transition state machine) in vid::Vid, built on the byte-exact tparm and the color Palette. Verified byte-exact against real ncurses for bold/underline/reverse and the ANSI color pairs, integrated into and courted via doupdate (NCURSES.DOUPDATE). Italic/altcharset/xmc/ncv and use_default_colors are not modeled.

vidputs_sp.note: Clean-room reproduction of vidputs/_nc_do_color (the SGR attribute + color-pair transition state machine) in vid::Vid, built on the byte-exact tparm and the color Palette. Verified byte-exact against real ncurses for bold/underline/reverse and the ANSI color pairs, integrated into and courted via doupdate (NCURSES.DOUPDATE). Italic/altcharset/xmc/ncv and use_default_colors are not modeled.

init_pair_sp.note: Registers a colour pair; pair_content matched to ncurses.

pair_content.note: Returns a pair's (fg,bg); matched to ncurses (incl. default pair 0 = 7,0).

pair_content_sp.note: Returns a pair's (fg,bg); matched to ncurses (incl. default pair 0 = 7,0).

has_colors.note: colors>0, derived from terminfo (tigetnum colors, court-matched).

has_colors_sp.note: colors>0, derived from terminfo (tigetnum colors, court-matched).

can_change_color.note: the ccc capability, derived from terminfo (tigetflag, court-matched).

can_change_color_sp.note: the ccc capability, derived from terminfo (tigetflag, court-matched).

keyname.note: Names a key code (printable/control/meta + the KEY_* table); matched to ncurses over -1..600.

keyname_sp.note: Names a key code (printable/control/meta + the KEY_* table); matched to ncurses over -1..600.

key_defined.note: The key a sequence is bound to (exact/-1 prefix/0), from the terminfo key caps; matched to ncurses.

key_defined_sp.note: The key a sequence is bound to (exact/-1 prefix/0), from the terminfo key caps; matched to ncurses.

has_key.note: Whether the terminal defines a key code, from its terminfo key caps; matched to ncurses.

has_key_sp.note: Whether the terminal defines a key code, from its terminfo key caps; matched to ncurses.

newpad.note: Creates a pad (a window with no screen association); geometry + is_pad matched by the geometry court.

newpad_sp.note: Creates a pad (a window with no screen association); geometry + is_pad matched by the geometry court.

subpad.note: Sub-pad at a parent-relative position; geometry matched by the court.

is_pad.note: Whether the window is a pad; matched by the geometry court.

prefresh.note: Pad-to-screen copy; shares the single-shape repaint seed (general doupdate is a seed).

pnoutrefresh.note: Pad-to-screen copy; shares the single-shape repaint seed (general doupdate is a seed).

pechochar.note: Add to the pad then refresh; the add is reconstructed, the echo refresh is the doupdate seed.

printw.note: Formats (C printf, delegated to the caller) then adds the string to the window; the window effect is addstr (courted via WINDOW.STATE).

wprintw.note: Formats (C printf, delegated to the caller) then adds the string to the window; the window effect is addstr (courted via WINDOW.STATE).

mvprintw.note: Formats (C printf, delegated to the caller) then adds the string to the window; the window effect is addstr (courted via WINDOW.STATE).

mvwprintw.note: Formats (C printf, delegated to the caller) then adds the string to the window; the window effect is addstr (courted via WINDOW.STATE).

vwprintw.note: Formats (C printf, delegated to the caller) then adds the string to the window; the window effect is addstr (courted via WINDOW.STATE).

vw_printw.note: Formats (C printf, delegated to the caller) then adds the string to the window; the window effect is addstr (courted via WINDOW.STATE).

addchstr.note: Writes a chtype array (char+attr) at the cursor without advancing, clipped at the margin; char+attr matched to ncurses via winch.

addchnstr.note: Writes a chtype array (char+attr) at the cursor without advancing, clipped at the margin; char+attr matched to ncurses via winch.

waddchstr.note: Writes a chtype array (char+attr) at the cursor without advancing, clipped at the margin; char+attr matched to ncurses via winch.

waddchnstr.note: Writes a chtype array (char+attr) at the cursor without advancing, clipped at the margin; char+attr matched to ncurses via winch.

mvaddchstr.note: Writes a chtype array (char+attr) at the cursor without advancing, clipped at the margin; char+attr matched to ncurses via winch.

mvaddchnstr.note: Writes a chtype array (char+attr) at the cursor without advancing, clipped at the margin; char+attr matched to ncurses via winch.

mvwaddchstr.note: Writes a chtype array (char+attr) at the cursor without advancing, clipped at the margin; char+attr matched to ncurses via winch.

mvwaddchnstr.note: Writes a chtype array (char+attr) at the cursor without advancing, clipped at the margin; char+attr matched to ncurses via winch.

newterm.note: Emits the init prologue; against the in-repo ncurses 6.4 pty oracle the composite framing diverges (emission order + feature extras), recorded not matched.

newterm_sp.note: Emits the init prologue; against the in-repo ncurses 6.4 pty oracle the composite framing diverges (emission order + feature extras), recorded not matched.

endwin_sp.note: Emits the teardown epilogue; same recorded framing divergence as initscr against the in-repo oracle.

exit_curses.note: Emits the teardown epilogue; same recorded framing divergence as initscr against the in-repo oracle.

wenclose.note: Whether a screen point is within the window; matched to ncurses.

mouse_trafo.note: Window<->screen coordinate transform with enclosure check; matched to ncurses.

wmouse_trafo.note: Window<->screen coordinate transform with enclosure check; matched to ncurses.

slk_set.note: Stores a soft label; slk_label read-back matched to ncurses.

slk_set_sp.note: Stores a soft label; slk_label read-back matched to ncurses.

slk_label.note: Returns the stored label truncated to the label width; matched to ncurses (80 cols).

slk_label_sp.note: Returns the stored label truncated to the label width; matched to ncurses (80 cols).

color_content.note: Default 8-colour rgb palette (can_change_color false on xterm); matched to ncurses.

color_content_sp.note: Default 8-colour rgb palette (can_change_color false on xterm); matched to ncurses.

extended_color_content.note: Default 8-colour rgb palette (can_change_color false on xterm); matched to ncurses.

extended_color_content_sp.note: Default 8-colour rgb palette (can_change_color false on xterm); matched to ncurses.

extended_pair_content.note: Extended (i32) pair (fg,bg); same registry as pair_content, matched to ncurses.

extended_pair_content_sp.note: Extended (i32) pair (fg,bg); same registry as pair_content, matched to ncurses.

init_extended_pair.note: Extended (i32) pair registration; same registry as init_pair.

init_extended_pair_sp.note: Extended (i32) pair registration; same registry as init_pair.

alloc_pair.note: Allocate/return a pair for (fg,bg); matched to ncurses.

alloc_pair_sp.note: Allocate/return a pair for (fg,bg); matched to ncurses.

find_pair.note: Find an initialized pair for (fg,bg) or -1; matched to ncurses.

find_pair_sp.note: Find an initialized pair for (fg,bg) or -1; matched to ncurses.

getch.note: The key-decoding half of getch is reconstructed: input::KeyMap::decode maps a buffered byte stream to the KEY_* code sequence ncurses' wgetch returns (longest-match trie traversal), verified against a real ncurses getch loop over a pty for arrows, F1-F12, navigation keys, and mixed text (NCURSES.INPUT). The live raw-mode read loop and the ESCDELAY timer for a lone trailing ESC are the ncurses-terminal crate's job (INP-02/TIM-02).

wgetch.note: The key-decoding half of wgetch is reconstructed: input::KeyMap::decode maps a buffered byte stream to the KEY_* code sequence ncurses returns (longest-match trie traversal), courted byte-for-byte against real ncurses (NCURSES.INPUT). The read-timing/raw-mode loop is deferred to the ncurses-terminal crate.
